DeFi-native users wanting EVM yield exposure through Canton's privacy 4. Other Canton apps integrating dvUSDCx as yield-bearing collateral 5. LP participants seeking spread-based returns on dvUSDCx/USDCx pool
reward_activities: 1. Deposit: USDCx CIP-56 transfer + dvUSDCx mint (2 on-chain tx) 2. Withdrawal: dvUSDCx transfer + burn + USDCx transfer (3 on-chain tx) 3. Share transfers: CIP-56 burn/mint between wallets 4. LP swaps: dvUSDCx/USDCx with fees 5. Third-party dvUSDCx usage (Track A — asset issuer markers)

summary: Cauri is a non-custodial wallet purpose-built for the Canton Network. It allows users to securely manage CIP-56 Canton Network assets while retaining full ownership of their private keys. It combines a simple interface with advanced capabilities, making it suitable for everyday use as well as more complex workflows. Cauri implements the CIP-103 DApp API to provide a consistent and secure way for applications to interact with user accounts without compromising custody or control.
expected_users: Cauri is designed for both retail and professional users who need a secure and straightforward way to interact with the Canton Network. It focuses on ease of use through an intuitive interface, while maintaining strong security with a non-custodial model and passkey-based wallet key encryption. The wallet enables users to manage assets and interact with dApps efficiently, offering a balance between simplicity for everyday use and the level of control expected by more experienced users.
title: Featured App Request: Lithium Digital Limited - Cauri

summary: Wintip is a decentralized tipping and red pocket platform on Canton Network. Users can send CC tips to any registered user and create red pockets (digital gift envelopes) that multiple recipients can claim. All transactions flow through a platform wallet using a two-step transfer pattern — sender transfers to platform, platform transfers net amount to receiver — with a 5% platform fee automatically collected. Features include user registration with automatic Canton party ID creation, real-time balance via Canton Validator API, tip history, profile pages with shareable tip links (/tip/username), and faucet distribution for new users. User testing: user: wintip-test-1 AND wintip-test-2 password: Password123!
expected_users: - Content creators, streamers, and influencers who want to receive CC tips from their audience - Community members who want to reward contributors with CC - Event organizers distributing red pockets to participants - Canton Network users exploring CC utility in social applications - New users onboarding to Canton Network via the built-in faucet
reward_activities: 1. Tip transactions - user sends CC tip to another user 2. Red pocket claims - user claims from a shared red pocket Each of these generates two on-chain Canton Coin transfers per event.
reward_type: Canton Coin - all transactions are CC transfers. Plan to integrate Activity Markers post-FA approval to maximize rewards.
daily_transactions: Estimated 2-5 transactions per active user per day (tips sent/received and red pocket claims). Each transaction generates 2 on-chain CC transfers.
title: Featured App Request: Winsnip - Wintip

summary: The first ‘Bring-Your-Own-Node’ Chrome extension wallet for Canton Network. Users connect through their own validator node — not a third-party RPC — for full sovereignty over their Canton interactions. Send and receive CC, manage participant identities, switch between parties, and connect to Canton DApps via CIP-0103, all from the browser. Also supports a managed mode where users without infrastructure onboard onto our hosted node instantly.
expected_users: Node operators who want a browser UI instead of building one. DApp developers who need a CIP-0103 wallet for their users. New participants without their own infrastructure who onboard via our managed node.
reward_activities: Activities in line with the Canton Foundation's current guidelines, such as movement of CIP56 tokens between parties, DApp interactions through the wallet.
reward_type: We would not use markers for users who bring their own infrastructure at launch. We would be the Provider on our onboarded users' Preapproved CC transfers. Simultaneously we would create Markers to offset costs of facilitating other CIP56 token movements, strictly within the guidelines set by the Foundation.
daily_transactions: 2-3 transactions for regular users, and 10-12 per day average for professional users.
title: Featured App Request: Benleo Group - Bitleo Wallet

summary: Goldacorn acts as a non-custodial matching and settlement engine bridging diverse liquidity pools. It facilitates: Retail P2P Crypto OTC: Using Daml-powered Escrow and Time-locks for seamless, trustless swapping of digital assets (e.g., Canton Coin to USYC) without relying on a centralized exchange holding user funds. Institutional Block Trading: Enabling large-volume crypto-to-crypto exchanges securely executed as Atomic Swaps directly on the ledger. Crucially, these trades are shielded from public mempools, preventing front-running and ensuring true execution privacy.
expected_users: Our specialized ecosystem unifies four distinct user personas: 1.Retail Traders (Tier 1): Everyday users requiring secure, low-barrier entry to swap stablecoins and major assets (e.g., USYC, cBTC) through simple P2P trades. 2.High Net-Worth / Institutional Traders (Tier 2/VIP): Enterprises and whales executing massive block trades who absolutely require zero MEV and fully confidential "dark pool" routing. 3.Liquidity Providers / Merchants: Regulated market makers who bridge the gap, managing high-frequency retail demands alongside deep institutional orders to maximize capital turnover. 4.Decentralized Arbitrators: Trusted, token-staked participants who resolve rare off-chain coordination or escrow disputes via multi-signature mechanisms.
title: Featured App Request: Goldacorn Protocol - Goldacorn Protocol

summary: CryptoLegacy acts as a Decentralized Guardian Network where users can publish "Liveness Bounties." Independent "Guardians" stake tokens to accept these verification tasks, earning Canton Coin by periodically interacting with users to verify their living status. If a user fails to respond after a grace period, their encrypted inheritance plans are automatically triggered for their designated heirs.
expected_users: The expected users of our application are individuals who hold digital assets (such as cryptocurrencies and NFTs) and are looking for a secure, privacy-preserving way to manage their inheritance. They are typically privacy-conscious users who want to guarantee their wealth is seamlessly passed to their chosen heirs without exposing their asset details to third-party platforms during their lifetime.
title: Featured App Request: CryptoLegacy - CryptoLegacy

summary: Redpacket allows users (creators) to lock digital assets into intermediary-free "smart envelopes" and distribute them across social media or communities. It primarily supports: 1.Diverse Distribution: Equal distribution capabilities and fully randomized cryptographic allocation (Lucky Packets). 2.Secure Verification Mechanisms: Claiming gated by hash passwords, or highly secure private peer-to-peer (P2P) targeted tipping. 3.Automated Lifecycle: Claimed funds are atomically settled directly into the user's wallet via the cip56 standard, and expired, unclaimed funds are safely and automatically returned to the creator via a refund mechanism.
expected_users: Our target audience primarily includes: 1.Canton Ecosystem Projects and Developers: Teams needing an interactive, viral tool for product launches, testnet airdrops, and AMA reward campaigns to attract new users. 2.DAO Community Managers and KOLs: Node community operators utilizing password-protected packets as trivia rewards or to incentivize deep content contributions. 3.Web3/Web2 End Users: Net-new users, brought in by viral social media sharing (Discord, TG, Twitter), completing their very first interactions and retention loops on the Canton Network.
title: Featured App Request: Redpacket - Redpacket

summary: Canton Prediction Markets is a curated weekly prediction market running entirely on Canton. Each week, the community votes on a single binary question from 3–5 candidates, stakes into Yes/No via a simple parimutuel pool, and a designated resolver settles the outcome on-ledger using DAML templates and USDC.x. The goal is to demonstrate visible ledger liveness through a ritualized cadence — vote → stake → resolve → recap — giving Canton stakeholders a clear, recurring heartbeat of real on-chain activity.
expected_users: Initial users are Canton ecosystem participants, community members, and Canton Network stakeholders who want to engage with prediction markets as a demonstration of ledger liveness. Senior Canton leadership and ecosystem developers are also expected to follow the weekly market cycle as a visible proof-of-activity signal.
title: Featured App Request: FireStar Digital - Prediction Market

summary: LynoBridge is the easiest way to bring Ethereum L2 liquidity to the Canton Network. Regardless of which token a user holds, they can receive USDCx on Canton in just a few steps: select a token, enter an amount, confirm. Relay Link handles the swap and CCTP handles the Canton transfer in the background; users never interact with this complexity directly. Thanks to the embedded wallet infrastructure, users do not need to set up a separate Canton wallet. The platform combines wallet creation and bridging into a single flow, allowing even non-technical users to complete the process within minutes. USDCx on Canton can be used directly in other ecosystem applications, settlement operations, and DvP mechanisms. LynoBridge makes Canton accessible to everyday users.
expected_users: LynoBridge’s primary target audience is individual retail users who hold liquidity on Ethereum L2 networks. The platform is designed for users who may not be familiar with the Canton ecosystem or its technical infrastructure but wish to move their assets to Canton. Over 12,000 pre-registrations and 850+ transactions during closed DevNet testing demonstrate concrete demand. In later stages, institutional users seeking to leverage Canton’s privacy-preserving infrastructure for tokenized asset settlement and inter-institutional transfers will also be onboarded.
title: Featured App Request: LynoBridge - LynoBridge

They urgently require high-frequency, highly available, tamper-proof, and accurate Settlement Prices to avoid cascading liquidation risks and price manipulation.

summary: Helios Canton Credit lets Canton Coin holders open CC‑backed credit lines, borrow stablecoins against their CC as collateral, and spend instantly via a virtual or physical card—without selling their CC. Users deposit CC on Canton, draw stablecoin loans, and use our credit card for everyday payments, while we share a portion of our Canton Featured App rewards back to them as usage‑based CC bonuses, creating a flywheel of real network activity, CC demand, and user adoption.
expected_users: Our initial users are: – Canton Coin holders who want to unlock liquidity while maintaining long‑term CC exposure. – Crypto‑native power users who prefer spending against collateral rather than selling and off‑ramping. – Early Canton ecosystem participants (validators, app builders, institutional users) who accumulate CC via rewards and need a credit and payments layer on top. We already have over 30,000 users on our waitlist, and our Canton‑enabled app is live on Mainnet in closed beta. We believe these users value regulated, institution‑grade rails, on‑ledger collateralization of CC, and transparent alignment between their real usage and the Canton Coin rewards they receive via our reward‑sharing program.

summary: CRoulettes is building a real game asset trading platform on the Canton Network designed for traditional gamers and independent game developers. Once integrated, in-game currencies and assets gain real tradable value, enabling players to buy, sell, and exchange assets on-chain while contributing real activity to the Canton ecosystem.
expected_users: CRoulettes targets three main user groups: traditional gamers, gaming community members, and independent game developers. Our initial users will come from the CRoulettes community who have already participated in testing and early product experiences. We have also established partnerships with gaming communities such as YieldGuild, Mob Guild, and GamingGrid, whose members will be able to join the platform after the Canton mainnet launch. Additionally, we are collaborating with several independent game developers with active player communities on platforms like Steam. With these integrations, we expect to onboard tens of thousands of real gaming users in the early stages and continue expanding as more developers join.

summary: Clareos is a settlement oracle for prediction markets on the Canton Network. Unlike existing oracles that focus on asset pricing for DeFi and tokenization, Clareos is purpose-built for prediction market resolution — publishing real-world outcome data and issuing immutable on-ledger settlement attestations as auditable proof of market resolution. It covers 70+ data feeds across 10 categories — crypto, equities, rates, commodities, FX, macroeconomics, Fed policy, sports, esports, and earnings.
expected_users: Prediction market operators on the Canton Network (e.g., Nexode, Unhedged). Operators run markets — such as FOMC rate decision outcomes, crypto price thresholds, CPI releases, or sports results — that reference Clareos data feeds. When a market's resolution window closes, the operator submits a SettlementRequest. Clareos verifies the outcome against its published data and issues an immutable SettlementAttestation — the on-ledger proof that the market was resolved correctly.
reward_activities: Settlement fulfillment — resolving a prediction market. When an operator submits a SettlementRequest and Clareos fulfills it, an immutable SettlementAttestation and a FeaturedAppActivityMarker are created together. This is the only activity that earns rewards. Routine data publishing does not generate markers.
title: Featured App Request: Lumarion Labs LLC - Clareos

summary: A digital property management platform that allows Dubai real estate investors to receive rent in real time and manage their properties remotely. The app includes practical tools designed for foreign investors, such as contract reminders, contract review support, rent price adjustment tracking, property performance visibility, and the ability to request home services from one interface. However, the core feature is real-time rental payouts, which solve a major inefficiency in the Dubai real estate market. Today, tenants are often required to provide 1 pre-paid cheque (for the entire annual rent) to secure better rental prices. This is inconvenient for tenants because it requires committing large amounts of cash upfront. Landlords demand this structure because they are concerned about liquidity and want to secure their rental income as early as possible. With our application, landlords are settled in real time (up to four times per hour) directly to their wallets, while rent is collected from tenants on a quarterly basis through standard post-paid cheque structures. This removes the liquidity gap for landlords and eliminates the need for tenants to prepay large annual amounts, improving cash flow efficiency for both sides without changing existing market behavior. One Estate will be earning revenue by charging a commission from both tenants and investors. From tenants - for a more convenient payments (in the end of 3 month rent, rather than 1 annual pre-paid). From investors - as an app “subscription fee”. Total commission per rental property is estimated at 6% (4% tenants + 2% investors). This will be used for social media/website descriptions 4

summary: FlowP2P is a peer-to-peer exchange platform, built for the Canton network, enabling individuals and institutions to make or take offers for Canton Coin and other CIP-56 tokens. The platform enables any individual or entity that uses the platform to make or take offers for Canton Coin or other eligible tokens - and to transact directly with counterparties to complete trades. FlowP2P allows direct transactions with peers and participants on the Canton network, at prices and volumes determined by the market - not by a single counterparty or centralized exchange. FlowP2P encourages on-ledger activity, reduces off-ledger settlement risk, and supports composability with future Canton apps and instruments. As Canton Coin adoption increases, participants require native, on-ledger mechanisms for price discovery and conversion that scale with network usage.
expected_users: Users are expected to be holders of Canton Coin and other CIP-56 tokens that wish to exchange between assets, without the overhead or opacity that some centralized exchanges, wallets and other options deliver. Participants and institutions wishing to convert CC to stablecoin, or from stablecoin to CC, will be able to do this in a straightforward and frictionless way. FlowP2P utilizes the atomic functionality enabled by Canton to remove counterparty risk, settlement delays and reconciliation challenges. Launchnodes has agreements to integrate FlowP2P with several existing Canton wallets, enabling seamless conversions between Canton token holders, at honest, transparent, market driven prices. FlowP2P provides a neutral, optional alternative venue for existing market makers, liquidity providers and OTC desks to view genuine market depth and pricing. FlowP2P delivers core financial market infrastructure for Canton Coin, co-existing with existing Canton trading, custody and bilateral arrangements.
title: New Featured App Request: Launchnodes

summary: Onchain Transfer Agent (OTA) finalizes issuance and ownership changes for tokenized securities on Canton Network. The application records ledger-final state transitions that create, transfer, or condition enforceable economic rights, providing an authoritative source of truth for tokenized ownership.
expected_users: Expected users include issuers of tokenized securities, stakeholders holding or transferring those securities, and external execution or settlement applications coordinating transactions whose finalized ownership outcomes are recorded by OTA.
reward_activities: OTA earns application rewards exclusively from finalized, on-ledger state transitions corresponding to: issuance finalization of tokenized securities, ownership transfers between parties, recording finalized settlement outcomes (Free-of-Payment and Delivery-versus-Payment), and application or release of protocol-enforced usage constraints on ownership units. Administrative, preparatory, off-ledger, or failed actions do not generate rewards.
reward_type: Activity Markers are emitted only after Canton ledger finality and correspond directly to finalized ledger transactions. Marker emission is proportional to the economic significance of the finalized action, subject to per-transaction granularity, minimum emission thresholds, and rate limiting. Full details are provided in CFA-0001 – Onchain Transfer Agent: https://github.com/jorisparis/canton-featured-apps/blob/main/CFAs/CFA-0001/CFA-0001-Onchain-Transfer-Agent.md
daily_transactions: Expected daily transactions per user are low and variable, as OTA records only finalized, economically meaningful events. Most users generate transactions episodically rather than on a daily basis, and transaction frequency reflects discrete issuance, transfer, or settlement events rather than continuous interaction.

reward_type: All pools and orderbooks will be paired with Canton Coin, we expect a lot of activity through atomic transfers on matching trades. Being trades add a lot of value to networks, we will be adding activity markers here and rebating some back to the users to encourage trading and bootstrap Cantex.
daily_transactions: "On a per-user basis, we expect most discretionary traders to submit ~1–10 trades per day, depending on strategy and market conditions. Liquidity providers are expected to interact less frequently, primarily when adding/removing liquidity or rebalancing positions. A small cohort of programmatic traders (bots, market makers, and API-driven strategies) will be materially more active as liquidity develops. Based on our TradFi and crypto experience operating high-volume trading platforms (including DEX and perpetual DEX venues), we expect a power-law distribution where ~5% of programmatic users generate ~95% of total trade volume and ticket count. In practice, this implies a small minority may execute ~100–1,000 trades per day, while the majority remains in the 1–10 range."

summary: xNS is the naming and routing layer for Canton. Instead of copying opaque Canton party IDs, users and institutions use names like alice.canton or desk1.fx.bankx.canton. Wallets and apps call xNS to turn those names into the correct Canton party and routing metadata before a transaction is sent. Relationship to CNS (and why xNS is not a CNS wrapper) CNS is the canonical, low-level party registry on Canton: “which legal/technical entity does this party represent?” xNS is an application-level naming and routing fabric: “what should this name mean today across wallets, desks, products and policies?” xNS uses CNS as an input but it is not a 1:1 alias or UI skin over CNS MVP (Loop + ENS connectivity via MetaMask) In the MVP, we have integrated with our partners at 5N Loop. A Loop user can: Claim a .canton name In Loop, a user searches for an available name (e.g. alice.canton), claims it, and sets a default destination (their Canton wallet). Policy checks and a reserved list apply. Send to names, not parties When anyone types alice.canton in Loop, the wallet calls xNS, which returns the current Canton party and routing metadata (derived from CNS where relevant). The user only sees and approves alice.canton. Link a .canton name to ENS via MetaMask The user connects an Ethereum wallet (e.g. via MetaMask), proves control of an ENS name like alice.eth, and links it to alice.canton. Respect reserved and sensitive names xNS enforces a reserved list and simple policies so obvious trademarks, existing institutional names and abusive terms are not available through the standard retail flow. These are handled via controlled enterprise or manual processes. Under the hood, only lifecycle events (create, update, transfer, verification, policy changes) are on-ledger. High-volume name resolution is off-ledger. Beyond the MVP, xNS adds structured enterprise namespaces, optional verification and richer metadata, but always as a separate application layer built on top of CNS, not a CNS re-skin. Beyond the MVP, xNS extends in three main directions: Enterprise namespaces: Structured hierarchies like bankx.canton, fx.trading.bankx.canton with policies for reserving, delegating and blocking names. Verification and richer metadata: Optional verification badges from recognised verifiers, plus basic metadata (e.g. supported products/roles) to make names safer and more informative. Governance and decentralisation: Gradual migration of key parameters (reserved lists, verifier sets, some marker logic) into governed on-ledger contracts, evolving toward a neutral, multi-stakeholder naming and routing standard for Canton.

summary: : Tokino is envisioned as a foundational building block for DeFi on the Canton Network. Its mission is to provide a core utility for token holders through an accessible token locking mechanism. By locking their Canton Coin (CC), participants are incentivized to hold their assets, which contributes to the network's long-term health and lays the groundwork for them to use their CC in a future liquid token state. The application's primary function is to increase overall user loyalty and engagement with the network, which in turn promotes token price stability and reduces sell pressure. This initial token-locking feature is designed as a foundational step. In the future, we plan to build on this by issuing a liquid receipt token (tCC) to users, which will serve as a key building block for a wide range of DeFi activities. For example, the tCC token could be used as collateral in lending protocols, as a liquidity source on decentralized exchanges, or deposited into sophisticated yield-generating vaults. The influence of protocols like Lido, ether.fi, and Ethena on the Ethereum DeFi ecosystem demonstrates the power of this model; their liquid tokens became fundamental, composable assets that catalyzed innovation in lending, trading, and yield strategies.
reward_activities: : The application will earn rewards by providing a premium, subscription-based "Proof of Trust" service. The reward-generating activity is the on-chain transaction that confirms a licensed and subscribed user is actively participating in the token locking program. This transaction, marked with a FeaturedAppActivityMarker, represents a paying user's ongoing commitment to the network's economic health and generates application rewards. We then share a portion of these rewards with the user on a bi-weekly basis. To prevent users from boosting their locked amount just before a payout and to ensure fairness for unlocking, *the reward calculation is based on the average daily balance of CC a user has locked over the entire 2-week period. ** The rewards are calculated using a tiered model designed to incentivize meaningful participation and long-term capital locking. This model includes a Base Reward Share: A subscribed user is eligible for the 10% base share of the application rewards generated by their subscription. While there is no minimum CC lock-in required to receive this, we are exploring linking eligibility to other requirements bound to active application use. This is to incentivize real adoption and not reward farming without interaction. It also includes a Locking Bonus: For every 2,500 CC the user locks (based on the bi-weekly average), their reward share increases by an additional 1% on top of their base share. (e.g., a user with 2,500 CC locked would earn an 11% total share: 10% base + 1% bonus). Finally, there is a Maximum Share: The total reward share a user can earn is capped at a maximum of 30%. **Disclaimer: Please note that all specific CC figures for reward tiers (e.g., the 2,500 CC for the locking bonus) are initial estimates. These numbers are subject to change as we refine the final token economic model. This model creates a powerful and transparent incentive for users to both subscribe to the service and lock their tokens, directly aligning their interests with the network's stability.
reward_type: : The activity directly involves Canton Coin. The per-round transaction for each licensed user will be marked with a FeaturedAppActivityMarker, ensuring it is recognized by the network as a reward-generating event. As a Featured App, these activities would generate rewards at a higher rate, which is critical for our user-centric model.
daily_transactions: : The baseline is 144 automated transactions per party per day, generated by the per-round fee mechanism on the locked subscription payment (6 rounds/hour * 24 hours). In addition to this baseline, the total transaction count will be higher due to occasional, user-initiated actions (locking and unlocking their CC principal) and the periodic bi-weekly reward payouts from our application to the user's party.
launch_date: : We are commencing development now and estimate a launch-ready application within a 1-2 month timeframe. Our launch is planned to align with the post-listing period of the Canton Coin to capitalize on its high visibility and immediately provide a stability tool for the ecosystem. The combination of these applications streamlines capital raising by combining issuance, compliance and settlement into a single workflow. Canton Coin rewards earned will help in real time complete the APPs while providing continuous improvement across the offering platform, the transfer agent and ATS. The Texture ATS will provide a regulated secondary trading venue for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) issued on the Canton Network. The ATS will match buyers and sellers through periodic auction mechanisms, execute trades, and settle transactions on-chain. It will enable institutional investors to trade digital securities with transparency and compliance.
title: New Featured App Request: Texture Capital Inc. (subsidiary of Texture Capital Holdings Corp.)

summary: : Texture Capital Inc is a subsidiary of Texture Capital Holdings and a FINRA member broker-dealer and operator of an SEC registered Alternative Trading System.Texture Transfer Services is a separate subsidiary that is an SEC-registered transfer agent. Our unique U.S. regulatory status allows us to compliantly issue digital securities and operate secondary markets. Today, Texture Capital already offers a issuer platform where subscribers can onboard and begin raising capital immediately. Featured App status will enable us to build this service natively for the Canton Network and to onboard a growing pipeline of real-world-asset issuers who aspire to operate on Canton. The subscription-driven transactions described above will provide early incentives and revenue that help complete the transfer agent service (Feature App #2) and support investment in the ATS for real-world assets on chain (Feature App #3). FA designation will accelerate delivery of this critical issuance utility to the Canton ecosystem. The platform streamlines capital raising by combining issuance, compliance and settlement into a single workflow. Canton Coin rewards earned will help in real time complete the APPs while providing continuous improvement across the offering platform, the transfer agent and ATS.

app_name: : Extension of the CBTC token decentralized-party app
summary: : CBTC is a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin token by BitSafe, designed for secure and decentralized bridging to the Canton Network. Each CBTC is CIP-56 compliant, and is fully backed by Bitcoin held in a secure, decentralized custody system powered by FROST (Flexible Round-Optimized Schnorr Threshold Signatures). The wrapped token is issued by a decentralized threshold of parties on Canton, and is held non-custodially by the user. This eliminates the risks associated with centralized custodians and traditional bridge architectures
reward_activities: : mint, burn, lock (encumber), unlock (un-encumber), and transfer
reward_type: : This application is an extension of the previous request we made for CBTC during an earlier cycle where we gated access to the token via credentials. That was granted to our own, controlled validator party. The decentralized party for CBTC, however, acts as a decentralized registrar. This party is responsible for issuing the CBTC token, leveraging the DA Utility Registry for token-standard compliance. Because of the system design, this party must be the one given Featured App designation for this token to be able to fire the marker on the events mentioned above. At the time of token launch, the CIP56 and featured app marker system wasn't launch and known to us, so it wasn't designed into the token this way initially.
title: New Featured App Request: Bitsafe

summary: Flowryd is a network intelligence and workflow orchestration application that accelerates on-chain market formation. It uses agentic insights to automate analysis for any participant to discover their network “DNA” using their unique position in the network to recommend complementary participants, actionable connections and activate networks on-chain. Unlike awareness platforms that broadcast 'who's here,' Flowryd operationalizes 'who do you need' - moving from noise to network formation.
reward_activities: : Flowryd will earn application rewards through user subscription fees and workflow automations confirmed on-chain at the Discover, Navigate and Activate phase. Revenue flows from discovering connections (subscription), navigating options (API access), and activating workflows (transaction confirmations) - the full Discover → Navigate → Activate lifecycle generates on-chain activity at each phase.
reward_type: : Canton Coin: transaction acceptance and workflow confirmation fees. Activity Markers: aggregate workflow classification for network-level analytics, maintaining full privacy of individual transactions and counterparties.
daily_transactions: : We expect app users to generate payment transactions from users every 10 minutes, i.e. 144 transactions per day.

reward_activities: : Mandalo will earn application rewards from a mix of core financial activities and daily utility: Primary activities * Investing in tokenized stocks: Each stock purchase or sale represents a meaningful, rewardable transaction directly on Canton. * Stablecoin issuance and circulation(using Brale): User deposits that settle into MNDUSD drive adoption of our Canton-native stablecoin, creating recurring activity on the ledger. Secondary activities * Spending via Visa card: Every card transaction debits MNDUSD from a Canton wallet, connecting on-ledger balances to real-world usage. * Peer-to-peer transfers: User-to-user transfers in MNDUSD support ongoing network activity and encourage stickiness within the ecosystem.
reward_type: : N/A — Mandalo does not use Activity Markers. All rewards are distributed directly in Canton Coin.
